content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

February 2, 2005

Maria E. Nunez
Post Office Box 733
Plymouth, CA 95669

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-05-010

Dear Ms. Nunez:

This letter confirms the receipt of your January 13, 2005, request for advice regarding a city council member's conflict of interest on an upcoming Indian casino issue. Under the provisions of California Code of Regulations section 18329(b)(8)(B), unless you are an authorized representative of the person for whom you seek assistance, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis.

Formal written advice may be requested when the name, title or position, and mailing address of the person whose duties are in question are provided. (Regulation 18329(b)(2)(A).) I have enclosed a copy of regulation 18329 for your review.

If you believe a violation of the Political Reform Act has occurred, you may file a complaint with our Enforcement Division (please see "Filing a Complaint," enclosed.)

Should you have any additional questions with regard to this matter, please contact the Commission at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

Enclosures
LM:jg

sections.question_synthetic: Can the FPPC provide advice regarding a city council member's potential conflict of interest on an upcoming Indian casino issue?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC cannot provide advice because the requestor is not an authorized representative of the person whose duties are in question; formal written advice requires the name, title, position, and mailing address of the person whose duties are being questioned.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination/rejection letter, not a standard advice letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections exist. The regulatory basis for declination is clearly stated. File number format suggests this may be mislabeled as 'W-05-010' (possibly indicating a withdrawal or similar status).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can the FPPC provide advice regarding a city council member's potential conflict of interest on an upcoming Indian casino issue?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC cannot provide advice because the requestor is not an authorized representative of the person whose duties are in question; formal written advice requires the name, title, position, and mailing address of the person whose duties are being questioned.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide advice on a city council member's potential conflict of interest regarding an Indian casino issue because the requestor (Maria E. Nunez) is not an authorized representative of the public official in question and did not provide the required identifying information.
